cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find the sum of all integer bases $ b > 9 $ where $ 17_b $ divides $ 97_b $. Hmm, let me start by understanding what this means. First, numbers in different bases. The notation $ 17_b $ means the number 1 followed by 7 in base $ b $. Similarly, $ 97_b $ is 9 followed by 7 in base $ b $. Since the base is $ b $, each digit represents a power of $ b $. Let me convert these numbers to base 10 to make it easier to work with. Starting with $ 17_b $. In base $ b $, this is equal to $ 1 \times b^1 + 7 \times b^0 $. So that simplifies to $ b + 7 $. Similarly, $ 97_b $ is $ 9 \times b^1 + 7 \times b^0 $, which is $ 9b + 7 $. So the problem is asking for bases $ b > 9 $ where $ b + 7 $ divides $ 9b + 7 $. In other words, $ (9b + 7) $ divided by $ (b + 7)
